title: Lively Indian Mussels Gulab Jamun
description: A briny, springy twist on the classic Gulab Jamun, using fresh mussels deep‑fried into sweet rose‑watered balls. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner that feels like a dessert.
instructions: Rinse the mussels under cold water and discard any that are open and do not close when tapped.
In a small saucepan, combine sugar and 30ml milk, heat until the sugar dissolves, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Sift the flour and semolina together into a mixing bowl and add the baking powder and cardamom powder.
Stir in the cooled milk‑sugar mixture, ghee, rose water, and grated coconut until a sticky dough forms.
Add the cleaned mussels to the dough, knead gently until the mussels are coated and the dough is uniform.
Heat vegetable oil to 180°C (356°F) in a deep‑fry pan.
Drop spoonfuls of the mussel dough into the hot oil, frying until puffed and golden brown, about 3–4 minutes.
Remove the fried mussel balls with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve warm, optionally drizzled with a light syrup made from the remaining sugar‑milk mixture.
